summary: This is a Tauri + React SPA with zero raster images in the UI layer. All icons are Lucide React SVGs rendered inline via JS. No tags, no CSS background-image, no external image resources. The only images in the repo are Tauri platform icons (app bundle assets, not served to browser) and test/QA screenshots (not part of the app). One actionable gap: no favicon is declared in index.html and the server returns 404 for /favicon.ico. No OG image exists. Grade A with minor recommendations.

images.svg_delivery: inline via lucide-react JS bundle (not file requests)
images.favicon_status: MISSING — index.html has no <link rel='icon'>, server returns 404 for /favicon.ico
images.og_image: MISSING — no opengraph meta tags in index.html

tauri_platform_icons.note: icon.png, 32x32.png, 128x128.png, 128x128@2x.png, iOS/Android variants present under src-tauri/icons/
tauri_platform_icons.impact: none — these are native app bundle assets, not served over HTTP

test_screenshots.impact: none — test artifacts only, never loaded by the app

issues.issue: No favicon declared in index.html and /favicon.ico returns 404
issues.recommendation: Add <link rel='icon' type='image/png' href='/favicon.png'> to index.html and copy src-tauri/icons/32x32.png to public/favicon.png (or convert icon.png to favicon.ico)

issues.issue: No Open Graph meta tags in index.html — sharing the web URL produces no preview card
issues.recommendation: Add <meta property='og:image' content='...'> and matching title/description tags if the app is ever shared as a web link (low priority for a local Tauri tool)

issues.issue: lucide-react.js is 926 KB uncompressed in dev mode (Vite dev server, no tree-shaking active at runtime)
issues.recommendation: In production Tauri build (vite build), Vite tree-shakes lucide-react to only the icons actually imported. This is not an image issue — it is a JS bundle concern. Verify production bundle with `bun run build && du -sh dist/assets/*.js`

passedChecks: Zero tags in all source files
Zero raster images loaded over HTTP
All icons delivered as inline SVG via Lucide React (no separate image file requests)
No large images (1MB+) in public/ directory
No WebP/AVIF needed — no raster images to convert
Test screenshots are test artifacts only, not served by the app
webpConversionGuide: N/A — no raster images present in the served UI layer. No conversion needed.
